Projects.title: Selected Projects

metadata.title: About
metadata.description: About page covering Furkan Cengiz's product, frontend, and server-side infrastructure work.

intro.title: I build web products across interface, backend, and infrastructure.
intro.subtitle: My work sits between product taste and operational discipline: usable interfaces, clean server-side flows, and systems I can keep running.

adventure.title: How I Got Here
adventure.p1: To give you a brief background, I initially pursued Industrial Engineering at AGU, but my passion for software development led me to build games instead. I then decided to focus on backend development first and later on, added frontend development to my skill set.
adventure.p2: After gaining some experience with Unity to develop games, I decided to learn .NET. The reason I shifted to web development is to build an online modern Turkish dictionary from scratch. The official dictionary lacks many features and I'm a fan of Turkish words and their roots. Initially, I developed multi-page applications but found it unsatisfying. Then, I discovered .NET APIs and became enamored with the simplicity of sending required data and using them however we want. Later on, I delved into client-side learning and studied HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Honestly, I struggled initially since I lacked proper resources. However, I was fortunate to be accepted into the Frontend Web Development BootCamp of Recoded.
adventure.p3: During the bootcamp, I not only improved my Frontend skills but also learned how to collaborate with teammates, provide feedback, manage time, and more. Furthermore, I honed my self-taught skills since our teachers encouraged us to self-teach.
adventure.p4: In this adventure, I made mistakes, and I will. I learned making mistakes is not what I should be afraid of. What I should be afraid of is being afraid of making mistakes which just means that I might not like what I do.

server.title: I am learning the parts that keep products alive after deployment.
server.description: I self-host the tools and apps I care about, then use that operational pressure to understand deployment, identity, backups, networking, and runtime behavior.

self-hosted.label: Self-hosted
self-hosted.title: My own apps in production
self-hosted.description: I run Vaultwarden, Turkish Dictionary, and this portfolio on infrastructure I maintain instead of treating deployment as somebody else's problem.

organization.label: Genç Türkler
organization.title: Infrastructure branch learning
organization.description: In the Infra branch, I am learning around real organization systems: portal deployments, Caddy, Docker Compose, PostgreSQL, LLDAP, backups, and Cloudflare tunnels.

direction.label: Next focus
direction.title: From feature work to operations
direction.description: I want to keep connecting product development with the server-side work behind it: safer releases, observability, access control, and reliable recovery paths.

server.note: The goal is not to become detached from product work. It is to understand the full path from interface decision to running service.

taken-courses.title: Selected Courses
taken-courses.description: Courses that shaped the stack, workflow, and product judgment behind the projects.
